title: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence
description: Introduction to the basic ideas and techniques underlying the design of intelligent computer systems. Topics include search, game playing, knowledge representation, inference, planning, decision making under uncertainty, and machine learning.

title: Data Structures
description: Fundamental dynamic data structures, including linear lists, queues, trees, and other linked structures; arrays, strings, and hash tables. Storage management. Elementary principles of software engineering.

title: Machine Learning
description: Theoretical foundations and practical methods for machine learning and pattern recognition. Supervised and unsupervised learning, neural networks, deep learning, generative models.

title: Calculus II
description: Techniques of integration; applications of integration. Infinite sequences and series. First-order ordinary differential equations. Second-order ordinary differential equations; oscillation and damping.

title: Linear Algebra and Differential Equations
description: Basic linear algebra; matrix arithmetic and determinants. Vector spaces; inner product spaces. Eigenvalues and eigenvectors; linear transformations. Homogeneous ordinary differential equations.

title: Introduction to Philosophy of Mind
description: An examination of the mind-body problem, consciousness, intentionality, and personal identity. Topics include dualism, physicalism, functionalism, and the nature of mental causation.

title: Ethics and Society
description: An introduction to the major ethical theories and their application to contemporary moral problems. Topics include consequentialism, deontology, virtue ethics, and applied ethics.

title: Introduction to World History
description: A survey of world history from prehistoric times to the present. Emphasis on comparative analysis of major civilizations and the dynamics of historical change.

title: The History of the United States
description: The history of the United States from Reconstruction to the present, including industrialization, immigration, labor movements, civil rights, and the rise of global power.

title: Introduction to Biology
description: General introduction to cell biology, genetics, molecular biology, physiology, and evolution. Lab sections provide hands-on experience with biological techniques.

title: Human Anatomy
description: Systematic study of the structure of the human body. Regional and systemic approaches to understanding gross anatomy, histology, and functional relationships.

title: Introduction to Economics
description: Introduction to economic analysis and its applications to contemporary problems. Supply and demand, market equilibrium, competition and monopoly, externalities and public goods.

title: Principles of Macroeconomics
description: Introduction to macroeconomic analysis: national income and product accounts, business cycles, unemployment, inflation, money and banking, fiscal and monetary policy.

title: Introduction to Art History
description: A survey of art and architecture from prehistory to the medieval period. Focus on visual analysis, cultural context, and the social functions of art across civilizations.

title: Modern Art
description: Art from the French Revolution to the present. Impressionism, Post-Impressionism, Cubism, Abstract Expressionism, and contemporary movements examined in their social contexts.

title: Introduction to Chemistry
description: Atomic and molecular structure, chemical bonding, thermodynamics, kinetics, and equilibrium. Laboratory work develops quantitative skills and introduces experimental chemistry.

title: Organic Chemistry
description: Structure, bonding, and reactions of organic compounds. Mechanisms of organic reactions, stereochemistry, spectroscopic identification, and synthesis strategies.

title: Physics for Scientists and Engineers
description: Mechanics and wave motion. Newton's laws, energy, momentum, rotational motion, oscillations, and waves. Emphasizes problem solving and physical intuition.

title: Electromagnetism and Optics
description: Electricity, magnetism, and optics. Electric and magnetic fields, electromagnetic waves, geometric and wave optics, and an introduction to quantum phenomena.

title: Shakespeare
description: Close reading of Shakespeare's major plays and sonnets. Examination of theatrical conventions, language, and themes in their historical and cultural contexts.

title: The Novel
description: History and theory of the novel from its origins to the present. Novels by major British, American, and world authors examined in their cultural contexts.

title: Introduction to Sociology
description: Introduction to sociological perspectives on social institutions, stratification, race, gender, and social change. Emphasis on applying sociological imagination to everyday life.

title: Introduction to Psychology
description: Survey of psychology covering biological bases of behavior, sensation and perception, learning and memory, cognition, development, personality, social psychology, and mental health.

title: Cognitive Neuroscience
description: Neural basis of cognition including attention, memory, language, and executive function. Emphasis on neuroimaging methods and cognitive neuropsychology.

title: Introduction to Political Science
description: Introduction to comparative politics, international relations, political theory, and American politics. Examines political institutions, behavior, and the nature of political power.

title: American Constitutional Law
description: The constitutional law of the United States. Judicial review, federalism, separation of powers, civil liberties, and equal protection. Analysis of landmark Supreme Court decisions.

title: Introduction to Music
description: Introduction to the elements of music and the art of listening. Examines diverse musical traditions including Western classical, jazz, world music, and popular styles.

title: Music of the World
description: Survey of music from Africa, Asia, the Middle East, Latin America, and indigenous cultures. Examines music as cultural expression and explores global musical interactions.

title: Introduction to Environmental Science
description: Introduction to environmental science including ecology, conservation biology, environmental policy, and sustainability. Examines human impacts on ecosystems and solutions to environmental problems.

title: Climate Change and Society
description: The science and policy of climate change. Physical basis of global warming, impacts on ecosystems and human societies, mitigation and adaptation strategies.

title: Introduction to Astronomy
description: Descriptive astronomy: the solar system, stars, galaxies, and cosmology. An introduction to modern astronomy for non-scientists, emphasizing physical principles and recent discoveries.

title: Galaxies and Cosmology
description: Structure and evolution of galaxies, dark matter and dark energy, the large-scale structure of the universe, and cosmological models. Modern observational techniques and results.

title: Introduction to Linguistics
description: Introduction to the scientific study of language: phonetics, phonology, morphology, syntax, semantics, and pragmatics. Language variation, change, and the relationship between language and mind.

title: Language and Culture
description: The relationship between language and culture. Linguistic relativity, language socialization, multilingualism, language and identity, and the cultural dimensions of meaning.

title: Introduction to Anthropology
description: Introduction to cultural anthropology. Social organization, religion, economics, politics, and world view in cross-cultural perspective. Ethnographic methods and the ethics of fieldwork.

title: Human Evolution
description: Introduction to biological anthropology and human evolution. The fossil record, genetics, primatology, and the emergence of modern humans and behavior.

title: Introduction to Computer Security
description: Introduction to computer security. Topics include software security, cryptography, network security, web security, and privacy. Students learn both attack and defense techniques.

title: Database Systems
description: Relational data model, SQL, indexing, query processing, transaction management, concurrency control, and recovery. Introduction to NoSQL systems and big data processing.

title: Operating Systems
description: Concepts of operating systems and system programming. Processes, threads, synchronization, scheduling, memory management, file systems, networking, and virtualization.

title: Computer Architecture
description: Engineering and design of digital computers. Instruction set design, processor pipelines, memory hierarchy, I/O systems, parallel architectures, and performance evaluation.

title: Introduction to Finance
description: Theory and practice of financial decision making in firms. Time value of money, capital budgeting, risk and return, capital structure, and dividend policy.

title: Marketing
description: Introduction to marketing strategy and management. Market analysis, consumer behavior, segmentation, targeting, positioning, and the marketing mix.

title: Organizational Behavior
description: Individual and group behavior in organizations. Motivation, leadership, decision making, communication, power, conflict, and organizational design and culture.

title: Introduction to Legal Studies
description: Introduction to American law and legal institutions. Criminal law, civil law, constitutional law, and the role of law in society. Emphasis on legal reasoning and case analysis.

title: Civil Rights and Civil Liberties
description: Constitutional protection of individual rights. Free speech, religious liberty, equal protection, due process, and privacy. Historical development and current controversies.

title: Introduction to Neuroscience
description: Introduction to the nervous system: cellular and molecular neuroscience, systems neuroscience, sensory and motor systems, and the neural basis of cognition and behavior.

title: Genetics
description: Principles of inheritance, gene structure and function, genetic regulation, genomics, and human genetics. Analysis of classical and molecular genetic experiments.

title: Introduction to Theater and Performance
description: Introduction to theater and performance as art forms and cultural practices. Scripts, staging, acting, design, directing, and the relationship between performance and society.

title: Dance Appreciation
description: Introduction to dance as an art form through viewing, discussing, and moving. Survey of major dance forms including ballet, modern, jazz, world dance, and contemporary performance.

title: Introduction to Film
description: Introduction to film history, theory, and criticism. Close analysis of films from different national cinemas and historical periods. Film language, genre, narrative, and ideology.

title: Statistics for Data Science
description: Probability theory, random variables, distributions, estimation, hypothesis testing, regression, and Bayesian inference. Applications to real-world data using Python.

title: Introduction to Statistics
description: Introduction to statistical reasoning for non-majors. Probability, sampling, estimation, hypothesis testing, correlation, and regression. Critical evaluation of statistical claims.

title: Urban Planning and the City
description: Introduction to urban planning, urban form, and the forces that shape cities. Housing, transportation, land use, sustainability, equity, and the politics of urban development.

title: Principles of Architecture
description: Introduction to architectural design. Form, space, structure, and material. Analysis of historical and contemporary buildings. Design studio with weekly projects.

title: History of Architecture
description: Survey of world architecture from antiquity to the industrial revolution. Greek, Roman, Byzantine, Islamic, medieval, and Renaissance architecture in their cultural contexts.

title: Foundations of Data Science
description: Critical thinking and quantitative reasoning applied to real-world problems. Programming in Python, statistical inference, visualization, machine learning, and social implications of data.

title: Human-Computer Interaction
description: Design and evaluation of interactive systems. User research, prototyping, usability testing, accessibility, and the social impact of technology design.

title: Privacy and Technology
description: Technological, legal, and policy dimensions of privacy in the digital age. Surveillance, data collection, social media, and the governance of privacy in networked environments.

title: Ancient Philosophy
description: Introduction to ancient Greek philosophy. The Pre-Socratics, Socrates, Plato, and Aristotle. Topics include the nature of reality, knowledge, virtue, and the good life.

title: Epistemology
description: The theory of knowledge. What is knowledge? Can we know anything? Topics include skepticism, perception, memory, testimony, inference, and the analysis of knowledge.

title: Introduction to East Asian Studies
description: Introduction to the history, literature, and cultures of China, Japan, and Korea. Examines key texts, social institutions, and cultural practices across East Asia.

title: Modern Japanese History
description: Japan from the Meiji Restoration to the present. Modernization, imperialism, World War II, postwar recovery, and contemporary Japanese society and culture.

title: History of China
description: Survey of Chinese history from the earliest dynasties to 1800. Political institutions, social structures, economic development, religion, and cultural change.

title: Introduction to South Asian Studies
description: Introduction to the history, religion, philosophy, and culture of South Asia. Hinduism, Buddhism, Islam, colonialism, and independence in the Indian subcontinent.

title: Introduction to Islam
description: Introduction to Islam: the Quran, the Prophet Muhammad, Islamic law and theology, Sufism, and Islamic civilization. Examines Muslim communities and practices worldwide.

title: Greek and Roman Mythology
description: The myths of ancient Greece and Rome as literature and cultural expression. Creation myths, hero stories, the Olympian gods, and the underworld. Influence on Western art and literature.

title: Introduction to Latin American History
description: Survey of Latin American history from pre-Columbian civilizations to independence. Aztecs, Incas, Spanish and Portuguese colonialism, the Atlantic slave trade, and liberation movements.

title: African American History
description: African American history from slavery to the present. Reconstruction, the Great Migration, civil rights movement, Black Power, and contemporary Black political struggles.

title: Introduction to Gender Studies
description: Introduction to feminist theory and gender studies. Gender, sexuality, race, and class as intersecting categories of analysis. Feminist politics and social movements.

title: Bioethics
description: Ethical issues arising from medicine and the life sciences. Informed consent, end-of-life decisions, genetic enhancement, research ethics, and justice in healthcare.

title: Political Philosophy
description: Classic and contemporary texts in political philosophy. Social contract theory, liberalism, democracy, justice, equality, and the nature of political authority.

title: Logic
description: Introduction to symbolic logic. Propositional logic, predicate logic, formal proofs, and the foundations of mathematical reasoning. Applications to philosophy and computer science.

title: Environmental Law
description: Legal frameworks for environmental protection. Environmental statutes, administrative law, common law remedies, and international environmental agreements.

title: Business Law
description: Legal framework for business transactions. Contracts, torts, property, agency, partnership, corporations, and intellectual property in the context of commercial activity.

title: Technology Law
description: Legal issues in the digital age. Internet law, intellectual property, privacy, cybersecurity, platform liability, and the regulation of emerging technologies.

title: Introduction to Electrical Engineering
description: Modeling, analysis, and design of information devices and systems. Circuits, linear algebra, machine learning, and signal processing with applications to sensing and communication.

title: Signals and Systems
description: Representation and analysis of linear, time-invariant systems. Fourier series, Fourier transforms, Laplace transforms, and Z-transforms with applications in communications and control.

title: Introduction to Civil Engineering
description: Introduction to civil and environmental engineering. Structures, geotechnical engineering, transportation, water resources, and environmental systems. Sustainability and design.

title: Thermodynamics
description: Fundamentals of thermodynamics for engineering applications. First and second laws, entropy, thermodynamic cycles, phase equilibrium, and chemical equilibria.

title: Introduction to Materials Science
description: Introduction to the science of materials: atomic structure, bonding, crystalline and amorphous solids, phase diagrams, mechanical and thermal properties, and electronic materials.

title: Global Health
description: Global health challenges including infectious diseases, chronic conditions, maternal and child health, and health disparities. Emphasis on low-income countries and global health governance.

title: Epidemiology
description: Principles and methods of epidemiology. Study designs, measures of disease frequency, causation, bias, confounding, and the applications of epidemiology to public health practice.

title: Introduction to Nutrition
description: Introduction to human nutrition: macronutrients, micronutrients, energy metabolism, dietary guidelines, and the relationship between diet and health.

title: Entrepreneurship
description: Theory and practice of entrepreneurship. Opportunity recognition, business model design, fundraising, team building, and the management of new ventures.

title: Supply Chain Management
description: Design and management of supply chains. Inventory management, logistics, procurement, demand forecasting, and global supply chain strategy.

title: Real Estate Finance
description: Financial analysis of real estate investment and development. Property valuation, mortgage markets, real estate investment trusts, and urban economics.

title: Introduction to Biochemistry
description: Chemistry of biological molecules: proteins, nucleic acids, lipids, carbohydrates. Enzyme kinetics, metabolism, gene expression, and molecular mechanisms of disease.

title: Environmental Chemistry
description: Chemical processes in the environment. Atmospheric chemistry, water chemistry, soil chemistry, and the fate of pollutants. Climate change from a chemical perspective.

title: Quantum Mechanics
description: The foundations of quantum mechanics. Wave-particle duality, the Schrödinger equation, quantum states, measurement, uncertainty principle, and applications to atoms and molecules.

title: Relativity and Cosmology
description: Special and general relativity, black holes, gravitational waves, and the origin and fate of the universe. Modern cosmology including the Big Bang, inflation, and dark energy.

title: Social Psychology
description: Social influences on thought, feeling, and behavior. Attitudes, persuasion, group dynamics, conformity, prejudice, altruism, aggression, and social identity.

title: Abnormal Psychology
description: Psychological disorders: their description, diagnosis, causes, and treatment. Anxiety disorders, mood disorders, schizophrenia, personality disorders, and substance abuse.

title: Child Development
description: The development of children from birth through adolescence. Cognitive, social, emotional, and moral development. Developmental theories, research methods, and applications.

title: World Literature
description: Major works of world literature in translation. Epic poetry, tragedy, the novel, and short fiction from Europe, Asia, Africa, and the Americas examined in comparative perspective.

title: Creative Writing
description: Workshop in creative writing. Students write and critique fiction, poetry, and creative nonfiction. Emphasis on revision, voice, craft, and the development of a personal style.

title: Contemporary Issues in International Relations
description: Contemporary challenges in world politics. Nuclear proliferation, terrorism, climate change, global governance, and the future of the liberal international order.

title: Democracy and Authoritarianism
description: Comparative analysis of democratic and authoritarian regimes. Democratization, democratic backsliding, electoral authoritarianism, and the conditions for stable democracy.

title: Introduction to Journalism
description: Principles and practice of journalism. Reporting, writing, editing, and media ethics. Survey of the changing media landscape and the role of journalism in democracy.

title: Digital Journalism
description: Storytelling in the digital age. Multimedia reporting, data journalism, social media, audience analytics, and the business models of digital news organizations.

title: Introduction to Geology
description: Introduction to geology: the structure and composition of the Earth, plate tectonics, earthquakes, volcanoes, rocks and minerals, geologic time, and the history of life.

title: Climate Science
description: Physical basis of climate and climate change. Energy balance, atmospheric and ocean circulation, the carbon cycle, climate feedbacks, and projections of future climate.

title: Introduction to Geophysics
description: Physics of the solid Earth. Seismology, gravity, geomagnetism, heat flow, and the dynamics of the Earth's interior. Seismic waves and the structure of the Earth.

title: Nature and Human Society
description: Introduction to geographic perspectives on nature-society interactions. Climate, ecosystems, resources, land use, and the uneven geographies of development and environmental change.

title: Urban Geography
description: Structure and dynamics of urban areas. Land use, housing, transportation, suburbanization, gentrification, and inequality in cities. Case studies from North America and globally.

title: Introduction to Cognitive Science
description: Introduction to the interdisciplinary study of mind. Perception, attention, memory, language, reasoning, and consciousness from psychological, computational, and neuroscientific perspectives.

title: Language and Thought
description: Does language shape thought? Cross-linguistic research on color, space, time, number, and social categories. Linguistic relativity, language acquisition, and conceptual development.

title: Introduction to Indian Civilization
description: Survey of Indian civilization from the Indus Valley to the Mughal period. Hinduism, Buddhism, Jainism, Sanskrit literature, art, architecture, and philosophical traditions.

title: Introduction to African Studies
description: Introduction to African history, culture, and politics. Pre-colonial Africa, the transatlantic slave trade, colonialism, independence movements, and contemporary challenges.

title: The Bible as Literature
description: The Hebrew Bible read as literary art. Narrative style, poetic technique, imagery, and characterization. Genesis, Exodus, the prophets, Psalms, Job, and wisdom literature.

title: History of Science
description: History of Western science from antiquity to the present. Scientific revolution, Enlightenment, Darwin, Einstein, and the development of modern science and technology.

title: Technology and Society
description: The relationship between technology and society from the Industrial Revolution to Silicon Valley. Technology, capitalism, labor, culture, and the politics of innovation.

title: Introduction to Public Policy
description: Introduction to the analysis and formation of public policy. Policy process, policy analysis tools, welfare economics, market failures, and case studies in health, education, and environment.

title: Behavioral Economics
description: Behavioral economics and the psychology of decision making. Bounded rationality, present bias, social preferences, nudges, and applications to policy and business.

title: Development Economics
description: Economics of developing countries. Poverty, inequality, institutions, technology, agriculture, health, and education. Field experiments and evidence-based approaches to development.

title: Introduction to Robotics
description: Introduction to robotics. Robot kinematics, dynamics, control, sensing, and planning. Applications to manipulation, locomotion, and autonomous systems.

title: Computer Vision
description: Image formation, representation, and processing. Edge detection, segmentation, object recognition, stereo vision, motion analysis, and deep learning for vision.

title: Natural Language Processing
description: Algorithms for natural language processing. Language modeling, parsing, machine translation, sentiment analysis, question answering, and large language models.

title: Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
description: Ethical challenges posed by artificial intelligence. Fairness, accountability, transparency, privacy, safety, and the societal impacts of AI systems.

title: Introduction to Cryptography
description: Foundations of cryptography. One-way functions, pseudorandomness, private-key encryption, public-key encryption, digital signatures, and zero-knowledge proofs.

title: Distributed Systems
description: Design and implementation of distributed systems. Consistency, replication, fault tolerance, concurrency, and performance. Case studies of large-scale systems including cloud computing.

title: Sustainable Development
description: Business strategies for sustainable development. Corporate environmental management, sustainable supply chains, ESG investing, and the transition to a low-carbon economy.

title: Introduction to Sociology of Education
description: Sociological perspectives on education. Inequality and schooling, race and education, educational reform, and the relationship between education and social mobility.

title: Sports and Society
description: Sociological analysis of sport. Race, gender, class, and nationalism in sports culture. Athletic labor, commercialization, and the politics of sport in American society.

title: Mind and Machine
description: Can machines think? The relationship between mind and computation. The Turing test, connectionism, consciousness, intentionality, and the limits of artificial intelligence.

title: Visual Communication
description: Principles of visual communication and design for journalism. Photography, infographics, data visualization, video, and the design of news products.

title: Astronomy and Astrophysics
description: Stellar structure and evolution, compact objects, the interstellar medium, and the formation and evolution of galaxies. Quantitative treatment using physics and mathematics.

title: Comparative Law
description: Comparative analysis of legal systems. Common law, civil law, Islamic law, and customary law. Courts, legal culture, and the relationship between law and society across nations.

title: Criminal Justice
description: The criminal justice system in America. Policing, prosecution, courts, sentencing, and incarceration. Mass incarceration, racial disparities, and reform movements.

title: Feminist Legal Theory
description: Feminist perspectives on law. Sex discrimination, reproductive rights, domestic violence, sexual harassment, and the ways law constructs and reflects gender.

title: Health and Social Behavior
description: Social determinants of health. Social class, race, gender, stress, social support, health behaviors, and the relationship between social conditions and health outcomes.

title: Introduction to Bioinformatics
description: Computational approaches to biological problems. Sequence alignment, gene finding, protein structure prediction, genome assembly, and analysis of high-throughput biological data.

title: Synthetic Biology
description: Design and construction of new biological parts, devices, and systems. Genetic circuit design, metabolic engineering, protein engineering, and applications in medicine and industry.

title: Introduction to Chemical Engineering
description: Introduction to chemical engineering. Mass and energy balances, transport phenomena, reactor design, separation processes, and the chemical engineering profession.

title: Energy and Society
description: Energy resources, technologies, and policies. Fossil fuels, renewables, nuclear, and the transition to a sustainable energy system. Economics and politics of energy.

title: Water Policy
description: Science and policy of freshwater resources. Hydrology, water rights, irrigation, urban water supply, water quality, and global water scarcity.

title: Philosophy of Science
description: Philosophical foundations of science. Scientific explanation, laws of nature, causation, confirmation, theory change, and the demarcation of science from pseudoscience.

title: Philosophy of Language
description: Meaning, reference, truth, and communication. Theories of meaning, the relationship between language and thought, speech acts, context-dependence, and conversational implicature.

title: Metaphysics
description: Fundamental questions about reality. The nature of time, causation, material objects, properties, modality, personal identity, and free will.

title: Introduction to Buddhist Studies
description: Introduction to Buddhism from its origins in India through its spread across Asia. The life of the Buddha, core teachings, meditation, ritual, and the diversity of Buddhist traditions.

title: Comparative Religion
description: Introduction to the major world religions. Hinduism, Buddhism, Confucianism, Taoism, Judaism, Christianity, and Islam examined in comparative perspective.

title: Introduction to Chicano Studies
description: Introduction to Chicano and Latino history, culture, and politics in the United States. Immigration, labor, civil rights, and the Chicano movement.

title: Introduction to Native American Studies
description: Introduction to Native American and Indigenous peoples of North America. Pre-contact history, colonialism, sovereignty, contemporary political issues, and cultural revitalization.

title: Game Theory
description: Strategic decision making. Normal and extensive form games, Nash equilibrium, backward induction, repeated games, and applications to economics, politics, and evolutionary biology.

title: Health Economics
description: Economics of health and medical care. Health insurance, hospital markets, pharmaceutical pricing, provider behavior, and evaluation of health policies.

title: Labor Economics
description: Labor markets and employment. Wages, employment, human capital, discrimination, unions, immigration, inequality, and the impact of technology on work.

title: Introduction to Contemporary Music
description: Music composed after 1945. Serialism, electronic music, minimalism, improvisation, and multimedia. Listening, score analysis, and the social contexts of contemporary musical practice.

title: The History of Jazz
description: History of jazz from its origins to the present. New Orleans jazz, swing, bebop, cool, hard bop, free jazz, fusion, and contemporary styles. Race, culture, and improvisation.

title: Introduction to Painting
description: Introduction to painting in oils or acrylic. Observation, composition, color mixing, paint application, and the development of a personal visual language. Weekly studio projects.

title: Drawing
description: Introduction to drawing. Line, form, space, light, and value. Observational drawing, the figure, and compositional strategies using pencil, charcoal, and other media.

title: Digital Art
description: Introduction to digital art making. Image manipulation, animation, video, interactive art, and net art. Conceptual frameworks for digital creativity and the history of media art.

title: Introduction to Photography
description: Introduction to photography as artistic practice. Digital and analog processes, light, composition, and developing a critical photographic eye. Weekly shooting and critique.

title: Sculpture
description: Introduction to sculpture. Three-dimensional form, space, and material. Casting, fabrication, and assemblage using a variety of materials. Site and concept in contemporary sculpture.

title: Environmental Policy
description: Environmental policy analysis and design. Market failures, externalities, pollution control, natural resource management, and the politics of environmental regulation.

title: Wildlife Conservation Biology
description: Principles of wildlife conservation biology. Habitat loss, fragmentation, invasive species, overexploitation, climate change, and strategies for protecting biodiversity.

title: Introduction to Ecology
description: Principles of ecology. Population, community, and ecosystem ecology. Energy flow, nutrient cycling, biodiversity, and the effects of human activity on natural systems.

title: Marine Biology
description: Biology of marine organisms and their environments. Marine habitats, adaptations, ecology, fisheries, and conservation. Lab and field activities at the Berkeley Marina.

title: Evolutionary Biology
description: Principles of evolution. Natural selection, genetic drift, mutation, gene flow, speciation, macroevolution, and the modern evolutionary synthesis.

title: Plant Biology
description: Introduction to plant biology. Plant cell structure, physiology, reproduction, development, genetics, and ecology. The role of plants in human civilization and global ecosystems.

title: Microbiology
description: Microbial diversity, physiology, and ecology. Bacteria, archaea, viruses, and fungi. Pathogenesis, microbial ecology, biotechnology, and the microbiome.

title: Virology
description: Biology of viruses. Viral structure, replication, genetics, evolution, and pathogenesis. RNA and DNA viruses, retroviruses, emerging viruses, and antiviral strategies.

title: History of Modern Europe
description: Europe from the Renaissance to the present. Reformation, Scientific Revolution, Enlightenment, French Revolution, industrialization, imperialism, World Wars, and postwar Europe.

title: The Holocaust
description: History of the Holocaust. The Nazi rise to power, anti-Semitism, ghettos, death camps, collaboration, resistance, and the aftermath. Memory, testimony, and lessons for today.

title: Cold War History
description: The Cold War from 1945 to 1991. Origins, nuclear strategy, proxy wars, détente, arms race, collapse of communism, and the legacy of the Cold War in contemporary international relations.

title: History of the Middle East
description: History of the Middle East from the rise of Islam to the present. Islamic empires, Ottoman period, European colonialism, nationalism, oil, the Arab-Israeli conflict, and contemporary politics.

title: Modern Russia
description: Russia from Peter the Great to the present. Westernization, revolution, Stalin, the Soviet system, the Cold War, collapse of the Soviet Union, and Putin's Russia.

title: Gender and Sexuality
description: Introduction to gender and sexuality studies. Theories of gender, sexuality, and the body. LGBTQ+ history and politics, queer theory, and intersectionality.

title: Race and Racism in America
description: Theories of race and racism in American society. Racial formation, structural racism, implicit bias, white privilege, and contemporary racial politics.

title: Internet and Society
description: Social, cultural, and political dimensions of the internet. Online communities, social media, digital inequality, misinformation, platform economics, and internet governance.

title: Data Ethics
description: Ethical dimensions of data collection, use, and governance. Informed consent, data ownership, algorithmic bias, differential privacy, and the ethics of data science.

title: Introduction to Indian Art
description: Survey of Indian art and architecture from the Indus Valley to the Mughal period. Buddhist, Hindu, and Jain traditions. Sculpture, painting, and temple architecture.

title: Chinese Art History
description: Survey of Chinese art from the Neolithic period to the present. Bronzes, ceramics, painting, calligraphy, and architecture examined in their cultural and historical contexts.

title: African Art
description: Survey of African art across the continent. Masks, sculpture, textiles, body arts, and architecture from sub-Saharan Africa and the diaspora examined in cultural context.

title: Introduction to Poetry
description: Introduction to poetry. Close reading of poems from various traditions and periods. Prosody, imagery, figures of speech, and the relationship between form and meaning.

title: American Literature
description: American literature from the colonial period to the present. Major authors and movements including Puritanism, Romanticism, Realism, Modernism, and contemporary writing.

title: Science Fiction
description: Science fiction as literary genre and cultural phenomenon. Utopia and dystopia, technology, artificial intelligence, space exploration, and the social imagination in SF.

title: Introduction to Immunology
description: Principles of immunology. Innate and adaptive immunity, lymphocytes, antibodies, vaccines, autoimmunity, immunodeficiency, and cancer immunotherapy.

title: Developmental Biology
description: Molecular and cellular mechanisms of development. Fertilization, cleavage, gastrulation, organogenesis, stem cells, and the regulation of gene expression during development.

title: Cancer Biology
description: Molecular and cellular basis of cancer. Oncogenes, tumor suppressors, cell cycle control, DNA repair, metastasis, and targeted cancer therapies.

title: Structural Engineering
description: Structural analysis and design. Loads, equilibrium, truss analysis, beam analysis, frames, and introduction to structural materials including steel, concrete, and timber.

title: Introduction to Nuclear Engineering
description: Introduction to nuclear science and engineering. Radioactivity, nuclear reactions, reactors, radiation safety, nuclear power, and applications in medicine and research.

title: Fluid Mechanics
description: Fluid statics and dynamics. Bernoulli's equation, viscous flow, boundary layers, turbulence, and applications to aerodynamics, hydraulics, and engineering design.

title: Computational Physics
description: Computational methods in physics. Numerical integration, Monte Carlo methods, molecular dynamics, finite element methods, and applications to classical and quantum mechanics.

title: Biophysics
description: Physics of biological systems. Protein folding, DNA mechanics, molecular motors, cell membranes, and the application of physical principles to living matter.

title: Classical Mechanics
description: Advanced classical mechanics. Lagrangian and Hamiltonian formulations, symmetry and conservation laws, small oscillations, rigid body motion, and special relativity.

title: Introduction to Number Theory
description: Elementary number theory. Divisibility, primes, congruences, quadratic residues, and Diophantine equations. Applications to cryptography and the Riemann hypothesis.

title: Abstract Algebra
description: Groups, rings, and fields. Homomorphisms, quotient structures, polynomial rings, field extensions, and Galois theory. Applications to geometry and number theory.

title: Real Analysis
description: The real number system, sequences, limits, continuity, differentiation, integration, and metric spaces. Rigorous treatment of the foundations of calculus.

title: Topology
description: Point-set topology and introduction to algebraic topology. Topological spaces, continuity, compactness, connectedness, and the fundamental group.

title: Probability Theory
description: Probability spaces, random variables, distributions, expectation, conditional probability, independence, law of large numbers, central limit theorem, and Markov chains.

title: Introduction to Cryptoeconomics
description: Economics of cryptocurrencies and blockchain. Bitcoin, Ethereum, DeFi, NFTs, smart contracts, and the macroeconomics of digital currencies and decentralized finance.

title: Introduction to Sociology of Law
description: Sociological perspectives on law and legal institutions. How law shapes and is shaped by society, legal consciousness, access to justice, and law in everyday life.

title: Mass Communication
description: History and theory of mass communication. Newspapers, radio, television, and digital media. Media ownership, journalism ethics, and the role of media in democracy.

title: Medical Anthropology
description: Cultural dimensions of health, illness, and healing. Medical systems, the social construction of disease, illness narratives, and the political economy of health.

title: Economic History of the United States
description: Economic development of the United States from colonial times to the present. Industrialization, labor markets, financial crises, the Great Depression, and postwar growth.

title: Social Movements
description: Theory and research on social movements. Civil rights, women's liberation, environmental, and labor movements. Political opportunities, resources, framing, and movement outcomes.

title: Digital Humanities
description: Computational approaches to humanistic inquiry. Text analysis, network visualization, geographic information systems, and digital archives applied to literature, history, and culture.

title: Introduction to Game Design
description: Principles and practice of game design. Mechanics, dynamics, aesthetics, narrative, and player experience. Rapid prototyping and playtesting of board games and digital games.

title: Parallel Computing
description: Parallel computer architectures and programming models. Shared memory, distributed memory, GPU computing, and performance optimization for scientific applications.

title: Quantum Computing
description: Quantum algorithms and complexity. Qubits, quantum gates, quantum Fourier transform, Shor's algorithm, Grover's algorithm, and quantum error correction.

title: Theory of Computation
description: Formal models of computation. Finite automata, context-free grammars, Turing machines, decidability, computational complexity, and P vs NP.

title: Philosophy of Mathematics
description: Philosophical issues in mathematics. The nature of mathematical objects, mathematical truth, proof, formalism, Platonism, constructivism, and the applicability of mathematics.

title: Introduction to Urban Sociology
description: Sociology of cities and urban life. Urbanization, community, neighborhoods, segregation, gentrification, and the social organization of urban space.

title: Introduction to Peace and Conflict Studies
description: Introduction to the interdisciplinary study of peace, conflict, and nonviolence. War, genocide, human rights, peacebuilding, and strategies for nonviolent social change.

title: Global Poverty and Practice
description: Understanding and addressing global poverty. Theories of development, humanitarian aid, microfinance, social enterprise, and immersive field projects in low-income communities.

title: Rhetoric and Social Change
description: Rhetoric in movements for social change. Abolitionism, civil rights, feminism, environmentalism, and contemporary activism. Persuasion, argument, and public discourse.
